steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks the student to ‘make and support a claim’ — a position about human arrival in North America backed by specific evidence from the notes. The notes document multiple pre-Clovis sites that contradict the Clovis First hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Arriving about 13,000 years ago, the Clovis were likely not the first humans in North America; items from more than a dozen sites suggest that humans had arrived before that time.": asserts the Clovis were likely not the first humans, then backs it with items from more than a dozen sites suggesting earlier arrival — exactly what a supported claim requires. ✓
  • "While the Clovis First hypothesis proposes that humans first arrived in North America around 13,000 years ago, humans had likely arrived before that time.": makes a claim but provides no specific support from the notes. ✗
  • "It has been claimed that the Clovis, arriving around 13,000 years ago, were the first human inhabitants of North America.": takes no position against it and makes no claim. ✗
  • "The stone tools found at the False Cougar Cave site should not be overlooked: they have important implications for the Clovis First hypothesis.": too narrow and too imprecise to constitute a well-supported claim. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
